case_title: ARRON MAURICE SMITH VS. VINH HUYNH

SMALL CLAIMS HEARING OF AUG-12-2022 - JUDGMENT.

THE COURT HAS ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF SMITH, ARRON MAURICE RECOVER FROM DEFENDANT HUYNH, VINH $10,000.00 PRINCIPAL, $75.00 COSTS, TOTAL JUDGMENT $10,075.00, DEFENDANT AS OWNER OF THE PROPERTY IS LIABLE FOR THE FIRE THAT CAUSED BOTH PERSONAL INJURY AND PROPERTY DAMAGE TO PLAINTIFF. TOTAL CLAIM EXCEEDED SMALL CLAIM'S LIMIT SO PLAINTIFF'S AWARD LIMITED TO $10,000. COSTS TO BE PAID TO COURT PER IFP. STAY OF EXECUTION 30 DAYS NO EXHIBITS FILED

ORDER OF EXAMINATION RETURNED SERVED, SERVICE DATE: JAN-11-2023 AS TO DEFENDANT HUYNH, VINH

NOT REPORTED. SMALL CLAIMS APPEAL HELD ON OCT-19-2022, IN DEPT. 606. THE SMALL CLAIMS JUDGMENT IS AFFIRMED. THE SMALL CLAIMS JUDGMENT OF AUG-12-2022 REMAINS THE SAME. RECORD REMANDED TO SMALL CLAIMS (DEFENDANT(1) EXHIBIT RETURNED TO SMALL CLAIMS DIVISION)

SMALL CLAIMS HEARING OF JUL-08-2022 - CONTINUED FOR INTERPRETER TO AUG-12-2022 AT 10:00 AM IN DEPT. 506. PLAINTIFF ARRON MAURICE SMITH IS PRESENT. DEFENDANT VINH HUYNH PRESENT. NO PROOF OF SERVICE ON FILE. DEFENDANT DESCRIBES SERVICE WHICH SEEMS TO BE PROPER. DEFENDANT WANTED TO WAIVE SERVICE ISSUES BUT WHEN I TRIED TO EXPLAIN HIS OPTION TO CURE SERVICE, HE DIDN'T UNDERSTAND; SO WE DECIDED HE NEEDS A VIETNAMESE INTERPRETER. NO EXHIBITS RETAINED. JUDGE: MICHELLE TONG; CLERK: R. SAHATJIAN. NOT REPORTED.(D506)

calendar.matters:
SMALL CLAIMS ORDER OF EXAMINATION CALENDAR
Off Calendar Jan-30-2023 Off Calendar

SMALL CLAIM APPEALS
Off Calendar Oct-19-2022 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:

Arron Smith, respondent/plaintiff, is present;
Gregory White, of Carbone, Smith and Koyama, appearing on behalf of appellant/defendant, Vinh Huynh, who is also present;
Hong Ha, Court Certified Vietnamese Interpreter (#300215), assisting appellant/defendant; and
Jeff Hazard, expert witness for appellant/defendant.

This case comes on calendar regularly for trial de novo. All parties and witnesses are sworn.

The Court advises the parties that the appeal is untimely, (under Code of Civil Procedure 116.750), and does not have jurisdiction. The parties discuss the matter.

The Court declares a recess.

The hearing recommences. After discussions, the Court confirms the appeal was filed late, (over 30 days) and does not have jurisdiction. The Court affirms the previous judgment.

Judge: Rochelle East; Clerk: Felicia Green; not reported; Dept. 606.
